config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama agar masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alat yang menekan orang per orang atas nama kepentingan bersama. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang adil dan tidak mengorbankan kebebasan dasar, martabat, dan perlindungan hukum individu. Kalau prioritas selalu dibalik, yang lemah justru paling mudah dirugikan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States we have seen how easily “the common good” can be used to excuse abuse of power. Individual rights need to be the default limit on what society or the government can demand, especially when speech, due process, privacy, or minority rights are at stake. That said, rights are not absolute in every situation, since public safety and basic order sometimes require real constraints, but those limits should be narrow and justified. If we flip the hierarchy too far toward society, we risk treating people as means to an end instead of citizens with inherent dignity.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju kalau kepentingan masyarakat ditempatkan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip utama. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, sementara yang paling terdampak justru orang kecil, minoritas, atau mereka yang suaranya lemah. Kepentingan sosial memang perlu, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar, prosedur adil, dan perlindungan hukum yang kuat. Kalau urutannya dibalik, mudah sekali masyarakat berubah menjadi alat untuk menekan individu, bukan ruang untuk melindungi martabat semua orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, history shows that once leaders claim they know what is best for everyone, it becomes very easy to trample speech, privacy, due process, and minority rights in the name of the common good. Society absolutely needs rules and some limits, but those limits should be narrow, accountable, and justified by real necessity rather than vague appeals to collective interest. If we reverse the default, the people with the least power are usually the first to pay the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Di Indonesia, alasan “demi ketertiban” atau “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan orang yang lemah, minoritas, atau yang tidak punya akses ke kekuasaan, dan itu justru berbahaya. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dibatasi oleh hak dasar, aturan yang adil, dan pengawasan yang kuat, bukan dijadikan alasan untuk mengorbankan martabat individu. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling gampang mengalahkan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi individu sambil tetap menjaga kepentingan umum secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general principle. From a United States perspective, we have too many examples of “the public good” being used to justify censorship, discrimination, and abuse of power, and once that door opens, the people with the least protection get hurt first. I do think society can impose limits for public safety, but those limits have to be narrow, evidence-based, and subject to strong legal checks. A healthy society protects the common good by protecting individual rights first, not by treating them as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
